violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety by RIDOH CFP at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Provided application to PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with food debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC relocated can opener to 3-bay sink to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025

The following items were observed held at (47-48) degrees Fahrenheit: cole slaw and various cheeses located in cold side station. Per interview with PIC cheeses and cole slaw were placed in unit 1 hour prior to inspection. PIC relocated cheeses and cole slaw to walk-in cooler. Re-temp the unit and temp was 41 degrees Fahrenheit at time of inspection. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025

Observed blue shavings from ice scoop hanging from nonfood-contact surface of area inside ice machine. PIC cleaned nonfood-contact surface at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener and chopper) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024

Vacuum packaged salmon was observed thawing with seal in tact. Vacuum packaged seafood must be opened to allow for adequate oxygen transfer when thawing. PIC cut open bags at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024

The walk-in cooler was observed with an accumulation of pooling water. Per interview with PIC, rain water was coming into the walk-in cooler presumably from the floor to wall junctures. PIC was instructed to move all food up a shelf to keep away from splash while water was disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2024
